message:
feat: Enhanced CLI tooling, operations documentation, and project tracking

## CLI Enhancements & Type Safety Improvements

### olmocr_cli.py Updates
- Removed unused typing imports (Any, Dict, Iterable) for cleaner imports
- Fixed type safety issue with `cast()` import now properly available
- Enhanced server detection logic with proper type casting:
  - Added `cast(str, detected_server)` to ensure type safety when using external server
  - Fixed `server_model_name` variable reference (was incorrectly `server_model`)
- Improved error handling and metrics display for remote server load monitoring
- Fixed undefined variable issues in image processing workflow

### mdwb_cli.py Improvements
- Enhanced `_poll_snapshots()` function with proper keyword-only parameters
- Added comprehensive `_watch_job_events()` function for NDJSON event streaming:
  - Supports cursor-based incremental polling
  - Configurable follow mode and polling interval
  - Properly handles empty event streams
- Expanded `events` command with new options:
  - `--since`: ISO timestamp cursor for incremental polling
  - `--follow/--no-follow`: Toggle continuous polling mode
  - `--interval`: Configurable polling interval (default 2.0s)
- Added `jobs watch` subcommand for continuous event tailing:
  - Full control over streaming parameters
  - NDJSON output to stdout or file
  - Proper follow mode with configurable intervals

### Smoke Testing Infrastructure (scripts/run_smoke.py)
- Enhanced command-line interface with new arguments:
  - `--dry-run`: Exercise pipeline without API calls
  - `--seed`: RNG seed for deterministic synthetic manifests
  - `--category`: Filter to specific test categories (repeatable)
- Improved main() function structure:
  - Better argument parsing with argparse.BooleanOptionalAction
  - Category filtering logic for targeted testing
  - Integration with olmocr_cli settings

## Documentation Updates

### Operations Guide (docs/ops.md)
- Added comprehensive nightly smoke run documentation
- Included detailed command examples with all available flags
- Documented `--dry-run` mode for testing without API access
- Added `--seed` parameter documentation for deterministic testing
- Explained `--category` filtering for targeted test runs
- Enhanced descriptions of output structure and manifest storage

### Project Plan Updates (PLAN_TO_IMPLEMENT_MARKDOWN_WEB_BROWSER_PROJECT.md)
- Updated Section 22 (Production Smoke Set & Latency Tracking):
  - Added implementation notes from PinkCreek (bd-ug0)
  - Documented run_smoke.py orchestration workflow
  - Enhanced description of --dry-run and --seed parameters
  - Added --category filtering documentation
  - Updated path structures for manifest storage

## Project Tracking & Coordination

### Bead Tracking Updates (.beads/beads.left.jsonl)
- Updated markdown_web_browser-27f: Enhanced coordination notes with Agent Mail reference
- Updated markdown_web_browser-bo2: Changed status to closed after event log fixes
- Updated markdown_web_browser-e1x: Closed after fixing olmocr_cli image preconvert issues
- Added new comment for markdown_web_browser-dwf: OrangeMountain taking CLI tailing work
- Added new comment for markdown_web_browser-y5b: WhiteSnow starting type-check regression fixes

### New Documentation (INITIAL_PROMPT_TO_GENERATE_PLAN_DOCUMENT.md)
- Added initial prompt document that generated the project plan
- Preserves historical context for project inception
- Documents the original requirements and vision

## Technical Improvements

### Type Safety
- Fixed Optional type handling in olmocr_cli.py
- Proper cast() usage for server URL type narrowing
- Enhanced type hints in mdwb_cli.py for file I/O operations

### Error Handling
- Better handling of empty event streams in CLI
- Improved feedback when no events are available
- Graceful fallback for missing server metrics

### Code Organization
- Cleaner import statements across CLI tools
- Consistent parameter naming conventions
- Improved function signatures with keyword-only arguments

This commit consolidates important tooling improvements, documentation updates,
and project tracking changes that enhance the development workflow and maintain
project coordination across the multi-agent team.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"markdown_web_browser-9s3","content_hash":"62d3de353f617e3ac920fcaa6ad5eedeadbb5926f4d3f7a208c7f8f373fb2a68","title":"Cache, manifest, and sqlite-vec persistence","description":"Sections 2, 10, 19.4, 19.6. Design content-addressed store, sqlite/sqlite-vec schema, manifest JSON contract, section embeddings API, and tar bundle export.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"BlackPond","created_at":"2025-11-07T12:02:34.395791717-06:00","updated_at":"2025-11-07T12:45:08.735073269-06:00","source_repo":".","labels":["embeddings","persistence","sqlite"],"comments":[{"id":11,"issue_id":"markdown_web_browser-9s3","author":"ubuntu","text":"2025-11-08 00:55 UTC — kicked off implementation.
- Reserved `app/store.py`, `app/embeddings.py`, `PLAN_TO_IMPLEMENT_MARKDOWN_WEB_BROWSER_PROJECT.md`, `docs/config.md`, and `docs/architecture.md`.
- Added progress note to PLAN §19.6 per instructions.
- Implemented new persistence scaffolding (RunRecord/LinkRecord tables, sqlite-vec initialization, tar bundle creation, storage config) + finished `app/embeddings.py` helpers.
- Updated config/docs/.env + pyproject (zstandard dependency) to match the new store behavior.
Next: wire Store into upcoming API endpoints and add tests once job orchestration hooks exist.
","created_at":"2025-11-07T18:51:18Z"},{"id":28,"issue_id":"markdown_web_browser-9s3","author":"ubuntu","text":"2025-11-08 01:05 UTC — persistence review fixes.
- `app/store.py`: `_apply_manifest_metadata` now understands both the legacy flat CaptureManifest dict and the new `ManifestMetadata` shape (environment/timings). We coerce any Pydantic model/dataclass/mapping into a plain dict and persist CfT/Playwright/viewport/FP8/timing information so ops dashboards work again.
- Added helper utilities (`_manifest_to_dict`, `_coerce_int`) to keep the conversion logic centralized and resilient to future schema tweaks.
- `app/capture.py`: added a Playwright channel normalizer so the default `PLAYWRIGHT_CHANNEL=cft` value no longer crashes `playwright.chromium.launch` (the CLI only accepts `chrome`, `msedge`, etc.).
Next up: wire the Store query helpers + embeddings search for bead `markdown_web_browser-to1` once schemas/settings/doc locks free up.

-{"id":"markdown_web_browser-bo2","content_hash":"b70de8002a3e8e69c742ebefabbb7bd6ccd96efa4c72e14ee48ea97bece62802","title":"Harden /jobs/{id}/events + JobManager event log","description":"Plan §4 requires the JSONLines feed + webhook log for each job, but the current implementation streams fresh snapshots only and attempts to json.loads() every SSE payload. That crashes as soon as a 'state' or 'progress' event arrives (not JSON) and we also drop all history, violating the spec's "retain 500 events per job" note. Fix the NDJSON endpoint so it emits valid JSON for every event, add per-job event log storage with a sliding window, expose it through the API, and cover the regressions with unit tests.","notes":"BlueCreek: starting deep review/fix for the /jobs/{id}/events JSON stream + JobManager event log retention. Investigating snapshot emission + NDJSON parsing failure now.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"BlueCreek","created_at":"2025-11-07T14:04:18.213023753-06:00","updated_at":"2025-11-07T14:04:23.120796635-06:00","source_repo":".","labels":["api","events"]}

@@ -0,0 +1,25 @@
+I have a weird idea for a project called markdown-web-browser
+
+First, read all of https://github.com/allenai/olmocr
+
+My idea is to take a headless chrome, say using playwright or something like that to make it easier, and then basically make a new UI for it like a browser with a URL bar, back and forward buttons, a google search bar, etc. But then it would pass this stuff to the headless chrome, load up the page (including JS and everything so it's all complete), and then save and image of the entire page from the headless chrome instance and seamlessly pass it to the olmocr model to turn into markdown. 
+
+The simplest implementation would use a remote API provider for the ocr model, as shown in the docs:
+
+```
+python -m olmocr.pipeline ./localworkspace1 --server https://ai2endpoints.cirrascale.ai/api --api_key sk-XXXXXXX --model olmOCR-2-7B-1025 --pdfs tests/gnarly_pdfs/*.pdf
+```
+
+(suitably modified to use image file like png screenshots (or maybe we would convert them to jpeg or webp first to save space, not sure what makes the most sense). Then we'd get back markdown which we would display instead of showing the original HTML. There would be a button in the UI where we could toggle between seeing the raw plaintext markdown with syntax highlighting OR the rendered markdown (rendered using something like the streamdown library). 
+
+Obviously this would be a pretty laggy experience, but we could have a clear progress indicator at the bottom status bar that would convey to the user exactly what's happening:
+
+1) Loading the website and rendering it in chrome
+2) Saving the screenshot to file and possibly converting to another format
+3) submitting the screenshot to the OCR API service for processing
+4) waiting for the response from the API
+5) Rendering the response.
+
+Every file processed in this way would be cached in a folder structure where each root URL would get its own folder, and each "page" could get a canonical filename based on the original url and saved as an .md file, and this could be saved in git to deal with the underlying html content changing without losing information. 
+
+Does this make sense? Basically this could be a powerful tool for use by AI agents because they wouldn't need native multi-modal/image/vision processing, just text, but it would still capture the rich semantic content of tables and other complex figures and stuff like that.  
\ No newline at end of file

@@ -699,7 +699,7 @@ Use these snippets as scaffolding for docs, onboarding, and regression verificat
 
 _2025-11-08 — PinkCreek (bd-ug0) spinning up nightly smoke + weekly latency automation and wiring manifests/log storage per this section._
 - `scripts/run_smoke.py` orchestrates nightly captures per `benchmarks/production_set.json` (writing `manifest_index.json` under `benchmarks/production/<date>/` and refreshing `weekly_summary.json`). See `docs/ops.md` for the runbook + verification checklist.
-- Use `--dry-run` to exercise the smoke pipeline without hitting `/jobs`; pair with `--seed` (defaults to `0`) to keep synthetic manifests deterministic so dashboard diffs stay stable. The script also mirrors the latest run into `benchmarks/production/latest_{manifest_index,summary}.json|md` so dashboards can read a stable path.
+- Use `--dry-run` to exercise the smoke pipeline without hitting `/jobs`; pair with `--seed` (defaults to `0`) to keep synthetic manifests deterministic so dashboard diffs stay stable. Scope to targeted slices with `--category <name>` (repeatable) when only certain sets need reruns. The script also mirrors the latest run into `benchmarks/production/latest_{manifest_index,summary}.json|md` so dashboards can read a stable path.
 
 Focus on a curated set of real customer-style URLs instead of synthetic benchmarks. Maintain `benchmarks/production_set.json` listing each URL, category, and target latency.

@@ -13,14 +13,19 @@ uv run python scripts/run_smoke.py \
   --http2 \
   --poll-interval 1.0 \
   --timeout 900 \
-  --seed 0
+  --seed 0 \
+  --category docs_articles \
+  --category dashboards_apps
 ```
 
-Add `--dry-run` when you want to exercise the pipeline without hitting `/jobs`
-(useful before the API is live or when secrets are unavailable). Pair it with
-`--seed <int>` (defaults to 0) so synthetic manifests remain deterministic.
-Dry runs still write manifests, summary markdown, and weekly stats so downstream
-tooling can be tested.
+- Add `--dry-run` when you want to exercise the pipeline without hitting `/jobs`
+  (useful before the API is live or when secrets are unavailable). Pair it with
+  `--seed <int>` (defaults to 0) so synthetic manifests remain deterministic.
+  Dry runs still write manifests, summary markdown, and weekly stats so downstream
+  tooling can be tested.
+- Use `--category <name>` (repeatable) to scope the run to specific categories from
+  `benchmarks/production_set.json`. This is useful when a particular slice is flaky
+  and you want to rerun it without exercising the entire set.
 
 - Loads `benchmarks/production_set.json` (docs/articles, dashboards/apps,
   lightweight pages) and runs each URL via `scripts/olmocr_cli.py`.
